evidence: External runtime dependencies: BlackLab (corpus search), FastJSON2 (JSON), SLF4J (logging). All are purposeful and non-overlapping. No two libraries serve the same role.

com.sun.net.httpserver is used directly in WordSketchApiServer (field type, method parameters) and HttpApiUtils. This is a JDK internal package — accessible in Java 11+ but not part of the public API contract. Projects using a different JDK distribution (e.g., GraalVM native-image, some cloud runtimes) may not have this package.

WordSketchApiServer.constructor creates SemanticFieldExplorer(executor) and passes it to ExplorationHandlers. SemanticFieldExplorer is not injectable; any test of WordSketchApiServer must bring up the full executor stack.
SemanticFieldExplorer.constructor creates CollocateProfileComparator(executor) internally. Same testability constraint applies.
BlackLabQueryExecutor depends on the BlackLab indexing, query, and results APIs — this is the project's core integration point and an appropriate heavy dependency.
GrammarConfigLoader depends on FastJSON2 for all JSON parsing. This is consistent with its use elsewhere (SketchHandlers, HttpApiUtils) — single library, no split.
ConlluConverter has zero external dependencies — pure Java I/O. Good.
LongIntHashMap has zero external dependencies but appears unused (no import found in production code), adding dead weight to the utils package.
pom.xml (not reviewed directly, but inferred): the BlackLab dependency likely pulls in Lucene transitively; no direct Lucene imports observed in production code except through BlackLab's API surface.

strengths: External dependency set is minimal and purposeful — no redundant libraries for the same concern.
FastJSON2 is used consistently as the sole JSON library across all files.
ConlluConverter and LongIntHashMap are dependency-free, appropriate for utility components.
dependency_health.issue_character: The main dependency health concerns are the use of a JDK internal API (com.sun.net.httpserver) as a first-class part of the server module, and the non-injectable construction of core collaborators that couples test paths to runtime dependencies.
dependency_health.score_rationale: The dependency set is lean and non-overlapping, which is a strong positive. The com.sun.net.httpserver usage is the most significant health concern — it is a platform risk that appears in multiple files including a utility class. The in-constructor instantiation of SemanticFieldExplorer and CollocateProfileComparator reduces testability but is not a dependency-import issue per se. Score of 74 reflects a healthy dependency structure with one concrete platform risk and one testability friction point.

issues.summary: com.sun.net.httpserver is used as a first-class API type in WordSketchApiServer and HttpApiUtils, creating a platform-specific dependency
evidence: WordSketchApiServer: private final com.sun.net.httpserver.HttpServer server; — stored as a field type
HttpApiUtils method signatures take com.sun.net.httpserver.HttpExchange as parameter type
Multiple files import com.sun.net.httpserver.* — this package is not part of the Java SE standard API
issues.suggestion: For the current development/research context, this is low risk. For production hardening, consider replacing with a lightweight embedded HTTP server that is part of the public Java SE API or an open-source library (e.g., Javalin, Undertow, or Jetty minimal). At minimum, confine the com.sun imports to one adapter file to minimize blast radius.

issues.summary: SemanticFieldExplorer and CollocateProfileComparator are constructed inside constructors rather than being injected, coupling dependents to runtime dependencies in tests
evidence: WordSketchApiServer constructor: new SemanticFieldExplorer(executor) — not injectable
SemanticFieldExplorer constructor: this.comparator = new CollocateProfileComparator(executor) — not injectable
issues.suggestion: Accept SemanticFieldExplorer as a constructor parameter in WordSketchApiServer; accept CollocateProfileComparator as a constructor parameter in SemanticFieldExplorer. Wire them in Main.java at the composition root.

root_causes: The com.sun.net.httpserver choice was likely a deliberate 'no extra dependency' decision for a research tool, and in that context is defensible. The non-injectable construction pattern is a recurring theme across the codebase.
